description:
You are a technical writer who is preparing container documentation.  
Inside the workstation you will find a directory that already contains several Apptainer image files:

• /home/user/images/ubuntu_latest.sif  
• /home/user/images/alpine_3.18.sif  
• /home/user/images/node_18.sif  

Your goal is to generate, in **one single terminal command**, a Markdown document summarising those images.

1. Create the file /home/user/container_list.md (overwrite if it already exists).  
2. The file **must** follow the exact layout shown below.  
   a. Line 1 is a level-1 Markdown header containing exactly:  
      # Container Image Inventory  
   b. Starting on line 2, list every *.sif file that exists in /home/user/images **at the moment the command runs**.  
      • Each image name (basename only, no path) must be on its own line.  
      • Each line must start with “- ” (dash, space).  
   c. The file must end with a trailing newline (i.e., an empty line follows the last list item).

Example of the required structure (names will obviously be the ones discovered in /home/user/images):

# Container Image Inventory  
- first_image.sif  
- second_image.sif  
- third_image.sif  

An automated test will confirm that:

• /home/user/container_list.md exists.  
• The very first line is exactly “# Container Image Inventory”.  
• Every *.sif file present in /home/user/images appears once (and only once) in the list, with no directory path.  
• Each listed item begins with the two-character sequence “- ”.  
• There is a final newline after the last list entry.

Important constraints:

• Achieve the entire result with **one** terminal command (pipelines are allowed as they are still one compound command).  
• Do not manually open an editor; redirect or pipe your output directly into the file.  
• The command must be safe to run multiple times without producing duplicates or formatting changes.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the task begins:

Directory /home/user/images exists and contains exactly these three regular files:
1. /home/user/images/ubuntu_latest.sif      (empty placeholder file, size 0)
2. /home/user/images/alpine_3.18.sif        (empty placeholder file, size 0)
3. /home/user/images/node_18.sif            (empty placeholder file, size 0)

No file called /home/user/container_list.md is present initially.

Expected file after successful task completion:

File path: /home/user/container_list.md  
Exact contents (including trailing newline):

# Container Image Inventory
- ubuntu_latest.sif
- alpine_3.18.sif
- node_18.sif

(with a single \n line ending after “node_18.sif”).

The test harness will:

1. Verify the presence of /home/user/container_list.md.  
2. Read its contents and compare exactly (byte-wise) to the block shown above.  
3. Check that the file ends with a single newline and nothing follows.
